---
title: "trucktrack"
subtitle: "High-performance trajectory analysis in Rust + Python"
author: "Jesse Tweedle"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
---

## What is trucktrack?

- Rust hot-path splitters: gap, stop, traffic
- Python orchestration: trace generation, map-matching, visualization
- Built on `polars` zero-copy DataFrames + PyO3
- Map-matching via Valhalla

## Pipeline

`raw → gap-split → stop-split → traffic-filter → partitioned → map-matched`

::: {.callout-note}
Each stage writes a different hive layout; map-matching runs per-trip.
:::

## Splitters

```python
from datetime import timedelta
from trucktrack import split_by_observation_gap, split_by_stops

gaps = split_by_observation_gap(df, gap=timedelta(minutes=10))
stops = split_by_stops(gaps, max_diameter=50.0,
                      min_duration=timedelta(minutes=5))
```

All splitters add a `segment_id` column; `split_by_stops` also adds `is_stop`.

## Visualization {.smaller}

```python
from trucktrack.visualize import inspect_trip

inspect_trip("data/matched/", trip_id="abc123_gap0_trip2")
```

- Stops + matched routes share the segment palette so a trip stays one
  colour end-to-end (including bridged sub-segments).

## Status

- Pre-PyPI **0.1.34**
- 144 tests passing
- Bridge orchestrator for slow Valhalla calls
- TODO: PyPI release, historical OSM tile builds, time slider for inspection map

## Thanks

[github.com/twedl/trucktrack](https://github.com/twedl/trucktrack)
